document.diagnosis: Iteration 28 favors selection replication and efficient answer-generating repair. The nine-bank one-selector system achieved the strongest family balance: 6/12 exact, 50% in every family, five helpful interventions, no harmful reversals, and conversion of six of seven base-oracle cases. The one-review five-bank repair also reached 6/12 with four helpful and zero harmful interventions, including one correct answer absent from its base bank. In contrast, the champion converted only four of six expanded-oracle cases, while regeneration increased coverage from five to six but finished 2/12 with one helpful and one harmful intervention. Thus candidate integration remains the main gap, while blind regeneration no longer merits an ordinary-batch slot without a different integration primitive.
extensions_requested: shared_evidence_board: Allow several independent reviewers to record concise candidate claims, contradictions, and verification results on a sealed domain-neutral board, followed by one final selector that sees the shuffled candidates and anonymized board. Freeze all roles before calls and never expose frequencies or the answer key.
most_important_evidence: The nine-bank one-selector mechanism is now 25/54 exact across four panels; its weakest-family point estimate improved from 6/14 to 8/18, or 44.4%. One unchanged replication would reach the five-panel, 60-case eligibility threshold.
On Iteration 28, nine-bank selection converted 6/7 base-oracle cases with five helpful and zero harmful interventions; its 50% accuracy in every family was the best weakest-family result.
Five-bank one-review repair converted a 2/12 base plurality into 6/12, with four helpful and zero harmful interventions, while using two fewer calls per case than the champion.
The established champion is now 137/324 exact after scoring 4/12 on Iteration 28. It remains the incumbent because the selector has not yet met the replication threshold.

Regenerate-then-repair raised oracle coverage only from 5/12 to 6/12 but selected 2/12, produced one helpful and one harmful intervention, and had 0% constraint accuracy. Its selector gap was four cases.
The proposed complete batch costs at most 204 Luna calls: 108 shared base calls from the nine-answer maximum bank plus 96 reviewer calls, leaving 96 calls of budget headroom.

proposals.hypothesis: Replicate the efficient repair ablation after it tied the panel leader at 6/12, created a correct answer missing from the base bank, and made four helpful with zero harmful interventions. The matched five-bank comparison isolates whether two additional independent repairers justify their cost.
proposals.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Reintroduce the previously tested sequential cross-examination architecture as the orthogonal lane. Against the same five-answer bank and three-call depth as the champion, it tests whether passing a falsifiable proposal and critique improves integration more than independent parallel repair.
proposals.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the current winner exactly. A fifth unchanged panel tests whether its 25/54 pooled result and 44.4% weakest-family point estimate survive enough evidence to qualify for formal comparison with the incumbent.
proposals.name: Nine-Bank One-Selector Falsifying Selection

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the established pooled champion unchanged as the principal replicated answer-generating repair control.
proposals.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the minimum-call independent baseline for panel calibration.
proposals.name: Direct One

proposals.hypothesis: Retain deterministic five-answer plurality as the shared-bank aggregation control.
proposals.name: Five-Vote Plurality

research_hypotheses: If the nine-bank selector preserves at least 44.4% weakest-family accuracy after this panel, its five-panel pooled record should be compared formally with—not automatically substituted for—the incumbent.
The five-bank one-review versus three-review matched pair will show whether reviewer depth adds exact accuracy or merely call cost and variance.
The five-bank cross-examination versus parallel three-review repair comparison isolates sequential critique from bank size and reviewer count.
Helpful and harmful intervention counts should remain primary diagnostics: exact gains caused by increased reversal of correct base answers would not support promotion.
document.risk_note: The selector is still provisional despite its favorable point estimate. A single fifth panel can satisfy the minimum sample threshold but remains vulnerable to panel variance; promotion should require pooled weakest-family superiority, correction balance, and no material partial-accuracy regression. Sequential cross-examination may propagate an early wrong proposal, so harmful reversals must be audited. No development-family result warrants a claim of generality before frozen evaluation on untouched problem families.
